_note: Illustrative traces demonstrating the four qualitative behavior categories discussed in the DeepSeek R1 paper and TinyZero. These are representative examples of the patterns, not direct quotes from a specific model run. Actual traces would be collected from saved completions during training for the final paper.

items.title: Random guessing
items.trace:
Using the numbers [4, 8, 15, 16] to reach 23. 4 + 15 = 19. Answer: 4 + 15

value = 19 (expected 23) · reward = 0

items.title: Structured attempt, no verification
items.trace:
Using [4, 8, 15, 16] to make 23. 16 + 8 - 4 + 15 = 35. That might work.
Answer: 16 + 8 - 4 + 15

value = 35 · reward = 0

items.title: Partial self-check
items.trace:
[4, 8, 15, 16] → 23. Let me try 16 + 8 = 24, close. 24 - 4 = 20. Not right.
What about 15 + 8 = 23? Yes.
Answer: 15 + 8

value = 23 · reward = 1

items.title: Explicit backtracking + verification
items.trace:
Given [4, 8, 15, 16], target 23. Try 16 + 4 = 20, plus 8 = 28, too high. Subtract instead: 16 + 8 = 24, minus 4 = 20, still not 23. Try 15 + 8 = 23. That uses two numbers and equals the target.
Answer: 15 + 8

value = 23 · reward = 1

items.title: Multi-step search
items.trace:
Numbers [3, 12, 7, 25], target 42. 25 + 12 = 37, need 5 more. 7 - 3 = 4, not 5. Try different pairing. 25 + 7 = 32, plus 12 = 44, minus 3 = 41. Close. What if 3 * 12 = 36, plus 7 = 43, minus 25 = 18. No. Go back: (25 + 12) + (7 - 3) = 37 + 4 = 41. Off by one. Try (25 - 3) + 12 + 7 = 22 + 19 = 41. Hmm. Let me try 3 * (12 + 7 - 25/...). Actually 12 + 25 + 7 - 3 + ... wait, I only have four numbers. Recheck: 25 + 12 + 7 - 3 = 41. One off. 3 * (7 + 25 - 12) = 3 * 20 = 60. No. Accept 41 is close; answer may need division. Not immediately clear, will settle: Answer: 25 + 12 + 7 - 3

(illustrative: model verbalizes backtracking, then settles on best candidate. value = 41 · reward = 0)

items.title: Verification after answer
items.trace:
[5, 10, 6, 2] → 22. Try 10 * 2 + 5 + 6 = 20 + 11 = 31. Too high. 10 + 6 = 16, plus 5 + 2 = 23. One off. 10 + 6 + 2 + 5 = 23 - still one off. Try (10 - 2) * (6 - 5) + ... wait. (5 + 6) * 2 = 22. Check: 5 + 6 = 11, times 2 = 22. Uses 5, 6, 2, all in the set. 10 unused is fine.
Answer: (5 + 6) * 2

value = 22 · reward = 1
